cards.title: The Trolley Problem
cards.body: A runaway trolley is headed towards five people. You can divert it to a side track, killing one person. What do you do?

cards.title: The Brain in a Vat
cards.body: Imagine your brain is in a vat, and your experiences are simulated. Can you ever be sure what's real?

cards.title: The Ship of Theseus
cards.body: If you replace every part of a ship, is it still the same ship? This thought experiment raises questions about identity and change.

cards.title: The Prisoner's Dilemma
cards.body: Two prisoners must decide whether to cooperate or betray each other. This dilemma illustrates the conflict between individual and group rationality.

cards.title: The Experience Machine
cards.body: Imagine a machine that can simulate any experience. Would you choose to spend your life in a simulated reality?

cards.title: The Philosophical Zombie
cards.body: A being that behaves like a human but lacks consciousness. This thought experiment raises questions about the nature of consciousness.

cards.title: The Mary's Room
cards.body: A person with complete knowledge of the world, but no experiences. Can they truly understand what it's like to experience something?

cards.title: The Grandfather Paradox
cards.body: What if a time traveler went back in time and killed their own grandfather? This paradox raises questions about the consistency of time travel.

cards.title: The Chinese Room
cards.body: A person who doesn't speak Chinese is locked in a room with Chinese characters. Can they truly understand the language?

cards.title: The Simulation Hypothesis
cards.body: The idea that our reality is a simulation created by a more advanced civilization. Is it possible to prove or disprove this hypothesis?
